  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Direct connection of copper to steel piping without a dielectric union or approved transition fitting",
    "Galvanic corrosion hazard due to dissimilar metals in contact"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Cut out the direct copper-to-steel joint",
    "Install an approved dielectric union or brass transition fitting between the copper and steel piping"
  ],
  "rationale": "Connecting copper directly to steel piping without an insulating barrier (dielectric union or brass spacer) causes galvanic corrosion, which leads to premature joint failure and leaks. This violates standard plumbing codes (IPC) and manufacturer installation requirements.",
  "references": [
    "IPC Section 605.24 (Connection of Dissimilar Metals)",
    "Manufacturer installation instructions for copper-to-steel transitions"
  ]
}
